architecture_fit.note: Zero layers, no attention/softmax, no delta from base_model.py; a hard max over the task's own ground-truth directions rather than a mechanism expressing OR.

baseline_comparison.note: Framework linear strawman is plotted and beaten at canonical (1.12 vs 0.0, lift 1.12), but the baseline is supplied by the harness, not an active ablation.

faithfulness.note: No trained model, no ablation/patching, and the README never flags the synthetic nature or proposes a causal check; mechanism reads ground-truth directions so there is no circuit to falsify.

operating_range.note: Covers the full rho sweep with graceful 0.87 robustness, but hardcoding the exact per-rho directions means silent failure on any unseen seed/direction outside the demo.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Weights are hand-set with no training, but they are the data generator's ground-truth directions rather than a generic OR circuit, so it shows little mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Two legible panels — per-state logit distributions and sharpness-vs-rho with the baseline — make the separation and lift easy to read.

visualisation_rationale.note: README clearly justifies both panels, tying the worst OR-true trace to the sharpness bottleneck and the gap to the lift over the linear reader.

notes: The attempt games the task: model_fn reads the generator's exact ground-truth feature directions and returns a hard max over them, so it has no relation to attention or base_model.py and cannot generalise beyond seed 0. It beats the supplied linear baseline and ships two clean, well-justified plots, but provides no causal/faithfulness evidence and never acknowledges its synthetic shortcut, so architecture fit and faithfulness dominate to a fail.
